content.text:
Here are some of the **top AI-related headlines appearing today, April 9, 2026**, based on current coverage from major tech/news outlets I could verify:

1. **Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text** — TechCrunch reports on a startup focused on making AI agents simpler for mainstream users. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))

2. **AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict** — TechCrunch highlights Amazon Web Services’ stance on backing multiple major AI players at once. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT** — TechCrunch says Tubi has launched a native ChatGPT app integration, showing AI’s growing role in media distribution. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline** — This was listed among the most popular AI stories on TechCrunch’s AI coverage page crawled yesterday. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/page/197/?utm_source=openai))

5. **OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek** — Published April 6, this remains one of the most prominent recent AI policy stories, outlining OpenAI’s proposals around AI-driven economic change. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/openais-vision-for-the-ai-economy-public-wealth-funds-robot-taxes-and-a-four-day-work-week/?utm_source=openai))

A quick note: I found strong, current AI headline visibility from **TechCrunch** today, but the Reuters/AP/The Verge search results returned less directly usable “today’s AI headlines” material in this pass. So this list is best understood as a **current snapshot of prominent AI headlines visible today**, not a ranked cross-outlet consensus.

If you want, I can do a second pass and give you:
- a **broader cross-source roundup** including Reuters/AP/The Verge/Bloomberg, or
- a **categorized summary** split into **companies, policy, products, and research**.
